title: Lowering Costs for Caregivers Act of 2025
short_title: Lowering Costs for Caregivers Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill makes it easier for people to help pay for their parents' medical bills. Starting in 2025, people can use money from special tax-free health accounts to cover their parents' healthcare costs. These accounts include Health Savings Accounts and other workplace health programs that many people already have. This change will help families take better care of their aging parents without paying extra taxes on the money they use for medical expenses.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.
plain_keywords: tax free money for parent healthcare
help aging parents with medical costs
adult children paying parent medical bills

sections.title: Section 1. Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Lowering Costs for Caregivers Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2. Health Savings Accounts
sections.text: This section changes the tax rules for Health Savings Accounts (special bank accounts that let people save money tax-free for medical expenses). Currently, people can only use money from these accounts to pay for medical expenses for themselves, their spouse, and their children. This law adds parents to that list. Now people can also use money from their Health Savings Account to pay for medical expenses for their own parents or their spouse's parents. This change applies to money spent after December thirty first, two thousand twenty four.

sections.title: Section 3. Flexible Spending and Health Reimbursement Arrangements
sections.text: This section changes the tax rules for two types of employer benefit programs. Flexible Spending Arrangements let workers set aside money from their paychecks before taxes to pay for medical expenses. Health Reimbursement Arrangements let employers give workers money to pay for medical expenses. Currently, these programs can only be used to pay for medical expenses for the worker, their spouse, and their children. This law adds parents to that list. Now workers can also use money from these programs to pay for medical expenses for their own parents or their spouse's parents. The money used for parents' medical expenses will not count as taxable income. This change applies to medical expenses that happen after December thirty first, two thousand twenty four.

sections.title: Section 4. Archer MSAs
sections.text: This section changes the tax rules for Archer Medical Savings Accounts (an older type of special savings account for medical expenses that is no longer available to new users). Currently, people can only use money from these accounts to pay for medical expenses for themselves, their spouse, and their children. This law adds parents to that list. Now people can also use money from their Archer Medical Savings Account to pay for medical expenses for their own parents or their spouse's parents. This change applies to money spent after December thirty first, two thousand twenty four.
